CONTENT.Club career: He graduated from Kumho High School, which is a youth team of Gwangju. He then joined Dankook University's football team.In 2017, Na joined the K League, where he signed with Gwangju FC.In 2019, he joined FC Tokyo.On 8 June 2020, he was loaned to Seongnam of K League 1.On 9 January 2021, Na joined fellow K League 1 club FC Seoul.For the 2022 season, he was appointed as the vice-captain of the club.On 4 January 2024, Na joined newly-promoted J1 League club Machida Zelvia on a permanent deal.
CONTENT.External links: Na Sang-ho at J.League (archive) (in Japanese)Na Sang-ho – K League stats at kleague.com (in Korean)
CONTENT.Career statistics: As of 26 May 2024Scores and results list South Korea's goal tally first.
CONTENT.International career: Na won the gold medal with the South Korea under-23 national team at the 2018 Asian Games.

SUMMARY: Na Sang-ho (Korean: 나상호; born 12 August 1996) is a South Korean professional footballer who plays as a winger or a forward for J1 League club Machida Zelvia and the South Korea national team.
